text.textcontent: Canada requires Electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) for all visa-exempt foreign nationals entering the country by air. The system is similar to the ESTA (Electronic System for Travel Authorization) currently in use by the U.S.
Visa-exempt foreign nationals, other than U.S. citizens, must have an eTA prior to being processed by Customs, Immigration, and Quarantine (CIQ) prior to entering Canada.
The following are exempt from the eTA requirement:

level1.textcontent: Individuals already in possession of a Canadian temporary resident visa
Travelers with a valid Canadian visa
Individuals in transit through Canada with a visa for the destination country

text.textcontent: Approved eTAs are good for five years or until the passport expires, whichever comes first.

video.alt-text: Electronic Travel Authorization - What you need to know
